9.reason: [When i=0, ch[i-1] accesses index -1, causing undefined behavior; also, the condition does not check if ch[i+1] exists (i+1 may be out of bounds)]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [When i=0, ch[i-1] accesses index -1, causing undefined behavior; also, the condition does not check if ch[i+1] exists (i+1 may be out of bounds)]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [When i=0, ch[i-1] accesses index -1, causing undefined behavior. Also, when '-' is at the end of the string, ch[i+1] accesses out-of-bounds memory.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  potential out-of-bounds access when i=0 or i is last index; also need to handle out-of-bounds.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Accessing ch[i-1] when i=0 causes out-of-bounds memory access; lacks boundary checks for i>0 and ch[i+1]!='\0'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Potential out-of-bounds array access when `i==0` or `ch[i]` is the last character; should verify `i > 0 && ch[i+1] != '\0'` first.

4.reason: [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers due to removal of 'gets' from standard library]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  gets is unsafe and may cause compilation error in C11; also no bound check.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  gets() is deprecated and removed in C11, which may cause compilation errors; use fgets() instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  `gets` is deprecated and removed in C11 standard, causing compilation errors in modern environments; use `fgets` instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; may cause compilation error with modern compilers due to removal of 'gets' from standard library]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; however, the actual compilation error likely stems from missing support for 'gets' in modern C standards (C11+), where it was removed. This causes a compilation failure if the compiler adheres strictly to newer standards.]

14.reason: This brace closes the inner if, but the outer if block is not closed before the else, causing a mismatched brace and a stray else.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  this closing brace ends the type-checking if; there is no else to print the dash when expansion condition fails.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  This brace closes the inner if, but the outer if block is not closed before the else, causing a mismatched brace and a stray else.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Missing `else` branch to print the `-` character when expansion conditions are not met, causing `-` to be lost in the output.

16.reason: Compilation error: due to missing closing brace for the outer if block, this else is unexpected. Also logical error: when expansion conditions fail, the dash should be printed, but here it is omitted.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Compilation error: due to missing closing brace for the outer if block, this else is unexpected. Also logical error: when expansion conditions fail, the dash should be printed, but here it is omitted.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  This `else` only corresponds to `if(ch[i]=='-')`, so `-` characters that fail expansion conditions are silently dropped instead of being printed.

15.reason: Missing else branch to print '-' when expansion conditions fail, causing '-' to be omitted from the output.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing else branch to print '-' when expansion conditions fail, causing '-' to be omitted from the output.
